analysis_result: Sonidegib did NOT significantly improve survival in STS-26T xenografts compared to vehicle. Median survival: Sonidegib = 39 days vs Vehicle = 31 days (8-day difference, 1.26x ratio). Log-rank p-value = 0.1357 (not statistically significant at alpha=0.05). This is concordant with the paper's statement of no significant benefit. Vehicle arm survival days: [27, 38, 33, 31, 29]; Sonidegib arm: [39, 28, 48, 28, 56].
denominator_used: N = 10 mice total (5 vehicle + 5 sonidegib) in STS-26T xenograft cohort from MOESM10 Fig 5l source data.
assumptions_made: All survival times treated as observed events (no censoring, i.e., event_observed=1 for all mice); days recorded represent time to pre-specified survival endpoint; two-sided log-rank test with alpha=0.05; sheet entries with variant spellings 'Sonedigib'/'Soniedgib' included under Sonidegib arm.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM10_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Fig 5l'
  - 20 data rows (S462TY vehicle x5, S462TY sonidegib x5, STS-26T vehicle x5, STS-26T sonidegib x5)
  - Columns: mouse ID label and 'Survival (days)' integer

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - Filter 1: rows labeled STS-26T -> 10 mice
  - Filter 2: split by treatment arm (Vehicle vs Sonidegib, including variant spellings)
  - Final analytic cohort: 5 vehicle + 5 sonidegib mice = 10

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Outcome: survival time in days (integer)
  - Group: Vehicle vs Sonidegib (Sonidegib includes typo variants 'Sonedigib', 'Soniedgib')
  - Vehicle values: 27, 38, 33, 31, 29
  - Sonidegib values: 39, 28, 48, 28, 56
  - No censoring indicator provided; all times treated as events observed

D. COMPUTATION
  - Median (numpy.median) per arm
  - KaplanMeierFitter (lifelines) fit per arm with event_observed=[1,1,1,1,1]
  - lifelines.statistics.logrank_test(vehicle_durations, sonidegib_durations, event_observed_A=1s, event_observed_B=1s)

E. RESULT
  - Vehicle: median = 31 days, mean = 31.6, range [27, 38]
  - Sonidegib: median = 39 days, mean = 39.8, range [28, 56]
  - Median difference: +8 days (Sonidegib > Vehicle)
  - Median ratio: 1.258
  - Log-rank test statistic = 2.2256, p = 0.1357
  - Interpretation: Not statistically significant (p > 0.05). Numerical trend toward longer survival with sonidegib, but confidence is low given small sample size (n=5/arm)
  - Concordant with paper's claim of no significant benefit

F. CAVEATS
  - Very small sample size (n=5/arm) yields low statistical power; effect direction favors sonidegib but fails significance
  - Assumed no censoring because source data provides single day value per mouse
  - Sheet had minor typos in 'Sonidegib' spelling (Sonedigib, Soniedgib) that were consolidated into one arm based on ordinal labeling
